claim_bullets.bullet_text: Paine argues that monarchy is contrary to scripture, as seen in Samuel's account of the people's request for a king (raw/1776ThomasPaine.pdf p.6-10).
He contends that hereditary succession is an insult to posterity, as all men are originally equal (raw/1776ThomasPaine.pdf p.6-10).
Paine claims that the first kings were often ruffians who gained power through force, not divine right (raw/1776ThomasPaine.pdf p.6-10).
He asserts that hereditary succession leads to poor governance, as it allows fools, wicked, and improper individuals to rule (raw/1776ThomasPaine.pdf p.6-10).
Paine argues that hereditary succession has led to civil wars and instability, citing England's history (raw/1776ThomasPaine.pdf p.6-10).

source_record_text: Thomas Paine's 'Document' from '1776ThomasPaine.pdf' (pages 6-10) argues against monarchy and hereditary succession, using biblical references and historical examples to support his position.
